weather.tip: Overcast and mild at 26.3°C this evening with only a 10% rain chance, so the commute home should stay dry — it'll cool to around 20.6°C overnight, so a light layer is enough if you're out late.

around.headline: Delivery agent who barged into Bengaluru woman's flat is arrested
around.summary: Police have arrested the man accused of forcing his way into a Bengaluru woman's home this morning to use her washroom and exposing himself; the incident was caught on camera. As always, hand off deliveries at the door only.

around.headline: Body found in Bengaluru park; police suspect murder
around.summary: A body was found in a Bengaluru park under suspicious circumstances and police are investigating it as a murder (reports vary on whether the victim was a pushcart vendor or a 20-year-old man, so details are still emerging). Avoid isolated park corners until police confirm more.

around.headline: Transport minister's incognito BMTC check exposes 'no change, get off' problem
around.summary: Karnataka's transport minister rode BMTC buses undercover and was told to get off by a conductor over not having exact change; he's ordered action against staff. Keep small change or use a digital ticket if you rely on BMTC, since fare collection is now under scrutiny.

around.headline: Wrong-side rider's near-miss with a truck goes viral, reignites traffic debate
around.summary: A video of a truck swerving at the last second to avoid a wrong-side rider in Bengaluru has gone viral and revived debate about lane discipline. Stay alert for oncoming wrong-side traffic near flyover ramps and narrow underpasses, especially at peak hours.

around.headline: Karnataka to roll out mobile forensic vans for faster crime-scene evidence collection
around.summary: New mobile forensic vans are being deployed to help Karnataka police process crime scenes faster, aimed at speeding up case resolution.

around.headline: MEA: 10 Indians rescued, one missing after vessel attacked off Oman amid Hormuz tensions
around.summary: As US-Iran tensions escalate over the Strait of Hormuz, India's MEA says 10 Indians have been rescued and one is missing after a vessel attack off Oman. A prolonged standoff in this key oil corridor tends to push fuel prices higher — worth watching if you're budgeting for petrol/diesel.

events.note: A Carnatic vocal concert by veteran singer Sudha Ragunathan as part of this annual Bengaluru music series.

events.note: A Bengaluru theatre artiste's solo homecoming show — a good pick if you're into intimate live theatre.

events.when: Underway this week, check venue for remaining match schedule
events.note: A city hockey tournament in progress — worth catching if you want live sport nearby.

events.name: Aero India 2027

events.note: Bengaluru's marquee airshow has its next edition confirmed — one to pencil into next year's calendar early.

street_smart.title: A vehicle is coming straight at you on the wrong side of a narrow flyover ramp, with a truck close behind you. What do you do?
street_smart.based_on: Real incident from today's feeds: a video of a truck swerving at the last second to avoid a wrong-side rider in Bengaluru went viral and reignited debate about lane discipline (NDTV / Moneycontrol).
street_smart.situation: You're driving down a narrow stretch near a flyover ramp in Bengaluru when a two-wheeler comes straight at you on the wrong side, weaving around slow traffic. There's a truck close behind you and no shoulder to pull into. What do you do?

choices.text: Swerve hard into the lane behind/beside you to dodge — you can straighten out once the wrong-side rider has passed.
choices.feedback: This is exactly how near-misses like today's viral Bengaluru video turn into pile-ups: a sudden swerve into a lane you haven't fully checked, with a truck close behind, removes the one predictable thing in the situation — your own path. The truck has far less stopping distance than you'd expect.

choices.text: Slow down steadily, hold your lane, and give the wrong-side rider maximum room to pass without any sudden moves.
choices.feedback: Correct. This is what actually worked in today's near-miss — the truck driver slowed and held space rather than swerving wildly, giving the wrong-side rider room to get past. Predictability, not evasive maneuvers, is what keeps two moving vehicles from colliding.

choices.text: Honk continuously and flash your lights to signal the rider to move, without changing your own speed or path.
choices.feedback: Alerting the other rider is reasonable, but doing it without easing off your speed leaves you no extra reaction time if they don't respond in time. Combine the horn with slowing down — don't rely on it alone.

choices.text: Brake to a full stop in your lane and wait for the wrong-side rider to navigate around you.
choices.feedback: An abrupt full stop can catch the traffic behind you off guard, especially two-wheelers following close. Slowing gradually is safer than stopping dead in your lane.

takeaways: When you spot a wrong-side vehicle, slow down and hold your lane rather than swerving — predictability protects everyone, including you.
Expect wrong-side riders cutting through tight spots like flyover ramps and underpasses — the tighter the space, the more likely someone's taking a shortcut against traffic.
Report recurring wrong-side hotspots to Bengaluru Traffic Police (WhatsApp helpline 94480 45900 or @BlrCityPolice on X) — repeated flags do get enforcement attention.

quiz.q: Karnataka's transport minister rode BMTC buses incognito this week. What problem did his surprise check expose?
options: A conductor refusing him a ticket over not having exact change
Severe overcrowding on the Whitefield route
GPS trackers not working on buses
quiz.why: The minister was told 'no change, get off' by a conductor during the check — BMTC has since been directed to act on staff and lean harder into digital/QR-code ticketing, which sidesteps exact-change disputes entirely.

quiz.q: Playback singer S Janaki, who passed away this week at 88, was widely known as the 'nightingale' of which film industry?
quiz.why: She sang in Kannada, Tamil, Telugu, and Malayalam across a career spanning decades, making her one of the most recorded playback voices in Indian film history.

quiz.q: India's Ministry of External Affairs said this week that 10 Indians were rescued (one still missing) after a vessel was attacked near which country?
quiz.why: The attack comes amid rising US-Iran tensions over the nearby Strait of Hormuz, a corridor that carries roughly a fifth of the world's oil shipments — instability there tends to move fuel prices.

quiz.q: Under Indian traffic rules, which lane should slower-moving vehicles generally keep to on a multi-lane road?
quiz.why: Overtaking is meant to happen from the right; slower traffic keeping left is exactly the discipline that reduces the wrong-side-overtaking situations behind today's viral near-miss video.

quiz.q: Bengaluru sits at an average elevation of roughly how many metres above sea level — one reason it stays cooler than many Indian cities?
quiz.why: At roughly 900m elevation on the Deccan Plateau, Bengaluru avoids the extreme heat of lower-altitude cities, which is part of why evenings like tonight's 26°C overcast weather stay comfortable.
